ruleset.name: Blades in the Dark
ruleset.edition: Blades in the Dark Core (John Harper / Evil Hat / One Seven Design, 2017)
ruleset.license: Original mechanics references; Blades in the Dark is published by John Harper / Evil Hat. The Forged in the Dark SRD is open-licensed; this bundle stays clear of trademarked Doskvol setting prose.
ruleset.summary: Forged in the Dark heist game. Roll N d6 = Action rating ± modifiers; highest die determines outcome — 6 = success, 4-5 = partial, 1-3 = failure, multiple 6s = critical. GM declares Position (Controlled / Risky / Desperate) and Effect (Limited / Standard / Great) BEFORE the roll; both axes negotiate before dice hit the table.

dice.notation: Nd6 take highest (Action rating dice pool)

resolution.description: Blades rolls N d6 where N = Action rating (0-4) plus modifiers (assist +1, push yourself +1 for 2 stress, devil's bargain +1). The HIGHEST die determines the outcome: 6 = success, 4-5 = partial (you get it but with cost), 1-3 = failure. MULTIPLE 6s = critical (extra effect or no cost). Action 0 means rolling 2d6 and taking the WORST (desperate fumbling). The GM declares Position (Controlled / Risky / Desperate — sets consequence severity) AND Effect (Limited / Standard / Great — sets how much the success advances the goal) BEFORE the roll. Both axes are negotiable in fiction; pushing, devil's bargain, and assist can buy improvements at stress cost. The dice widget runs in manual NdX mode; the GM agent prompt teaches all the moves and consequences.
resolution.noticeText: Blades uses Nd6-take-highest with Position + Effect. The widget is manual — see gm-agent for full resolution flow.

attributes.name: Insight
attributes.description: Quick-thinking, perception, study. Covers Hunt / Study / Survey / Tinker Actions. Resists harm to mind / sense / cleverness — Insight resistance roll.

attributes.name: Prowess
attributes.description: Physical capability, stealth, combat. Covers Finesse / Prowl / Skirmish / Wreck Actions. Resists harm to body / position — Prowess resistance roll.

attributes.name: Resolve
attributes.description: Willpower, conviction, social presence. Covers Attune / Command / Consort / Sway Actions. Resists harm to morale / heart / will — Resolve resistance roll.

skills.name: Hunt
skills.description: Track, ambush, attack at range. Catching prey before they escape.

skills.name: Study
skills.description: Examine details, decipher meaning, recall lore. Reading a person or text.

skills.name: Survey
skills.description: Observe a situation, predict events, look around. Casing a place.

skills.name: Tinker
skills.description: Fiddle with devices, pick locks, disarm traps, modify gear.

skills.name: Finesse
skills.description: Sleight of hand, riding, controlling vehicles, dueling with quick precision.

skills.name: Prowl
skills.description: Sneak, hide, move quietly, parkour through unfamiliar terrain.

skills.name: Skirmish
skills.description: Engage in close combat, fight savagely, brawl. Up-close violence.

skills.name: Wreck
skills.description: Break things, demolish, sunder. Smashing through obstacles.

skills.name: Attune
skills.description: Sense, channel, or contact the ghost field. Resist spectral / electroplasmic threats.

skills.name: Command
skills.description: Intimidate, lead, demand obedience. Force someone to act against their preference.

skills.name: Consort
skills.description: Socialize, schmooze, network. Negotiate from a position of mutual interest.

skills.name: Sway
skills.description: Persuade, deceive, charm. Convince someone to see things your way.

Controlled.description: Position: you act from a position of advantage. Failure costs minor — withdraw, reduced Effect, a small complication.

Risky.description: Position: you act in the danger zone. Failure costs standard — harm, lost opportunity, escalating trouble.

Desperate.description: Position: you act under dire pressure. Failure costs severe — serious harm, lost objective, fatal complication.

Limited.description: Effect: even success advances the goal only slightly. Use when conditions or scale work against the PC.

Standard.description: Effect: success advances the goal as the player intended.

Great.description: Effect: success advances the goal further than expected — extra clock ticks, bonus information, broader impact.

derivedStats.name: Position
derivedStats.formula: GM-set per roll. Controlled / Risky / Desperate. Negotiable in fiction.

derivedStats.name: Effect
derivedStats.formula: GM-set per roll. Limited / Standard / Great. Negotiable.

vocabularyHints.pattern: I attack / I fight / I shoot
vocabularyHints.hint: In Blades: pick an Action rating (Skirmish for melee, Hunt for ranged, Finesse for fencing-style). Roll dice = rating. GM declares Position (Controlled/Risky/Desperate) and Effect (Limited/Standard/Great) BEFORE the roll. Highest die: 6 = success, 4-5 = partial (with cost), 1-3 = bad outcome.

vocabularyHints.pattern: I sneak / I hide / I prowl
vocabularyHints.hint: Prowl action. GM should declare Position based on guards / lighting / distance. Effect typically Standard unless circumstances are unusual.
Player gains +1d to the roll OR +1 Effect OR perform an action you couldn't otherwise (e.g. supernaturally hard feat). Cost: 2 Stress. Mark stress on the sheet.
GM offers a complication (collateral damage, double-crossed friend, attention drawn) in exchange for +1d. Player accepts or declines. If accepted, the complication WILL happen regardless of roll.

After taking a consequence (harm, lost ground, breakdown), the player rolls the appropriate Attribute (Insight/Prowess/Resolve) as Nd6 take highest. 6 = avoid it entirely; 4-5 = reduce it; 1-3 = take it. Cost: 6 - highest die in Stress (so a 6 = 0 stress, a 1 = 5 stress).

Downtime activity. Roll the lowest action rating in the Vice's category (or just 1d). Result = stress cleared. Watch for Overindulgence — rolling more dice than current stress can trigger a Vice complication.

lorebook.name: Blades in the Dark core reference
lorebook.description: Keyword-triggered ruleset reference for Blades in the Dark's Action+Position+Effect resolution and Score/Downtime loop.

entries.name: Mechanic: action roll (Nd6 take highest)
entries.content:
An Action Roll resolves PC actions under uncertainty.

**Pool size:** N = the relevant Action rating (0-4), modified by:
- +1d Assist (an ally takes 1 stress to help)
- +1d Push Yourself (PC takes 2 stress)
- +1d Devil's Bargain (PC accepts a complication offered by the GM)

If the rating is 0, the PC rolls 2d6 and takes the WORST result (desperate fumbling).

**Resolution:** look at the HIGHEST die rolled.
- 6 = success — the action works.
- 4-5 = partial success — the action works, but with a cost (lesser consequence, complication, escalated Position).
- 1-3 = bad outcome — the action fails, and a consequence lands.
- Multiple 6s = critical — increased Effect or removed Cost.

entries.name: Mechanic: Position (Controlled / Risky / Desperate)
entries.content:
**Position** sets the SEVERITY of consequence on a failed or partial roll. The GM declares Position BEFORE the roll, based on fictional positioning.

- **Controlled** — you have the upper hand. Failure cost is mild: withdraw, reduced Effect, minor complication, optional retry.
- **Risky** — you're in the danger zone. Failure cost is standard: harm level 2, lost opportunity, escalating trouble.
- **Desperate** — you're under dire pressure. Failure cost is severe: harm level 3, lost objective, major complication, fatal escalation possible.

Position is NEGOTIABLE in fiction. Players can propose changes ("I'll spend stress to lurk in the rafters first — does that bring it to Controlled?"). The GM can also escalate Position mid-scene as circumstances change.

entries.name: Mechanic: Effect (Limited / Standard / Great)
entries.content:
**Effect** sets HOW MUCH a success advances the goal. The GM declares Effect BEFORE the roll, based on quality, scale, potency, and other factors.

- **Limited** — even success advances only slightly. Use when scale, gear, or conditions work against the PC (fighting a colossus with a knife, picking a finely-tuned lock with a butter knife).
- **Standard** — success advances as the player intended.
- **Great** — success advances further than expected (extra clock ticks, bonus info, broader scope).

Effect is also negotiable. Players can spend stress to push for +1 Effect; gear, allies, and tier/quality can also boost.

entries.name: Mechanic: Stress and Trauma
entries.content:
**Stress** (track of 9): the resource for pushing, resisting, and special abilities. Filled by:
- Push Yourself (+2 stress for +1d / +1 Effect / impossible feat)
- Resist a consequence (cost = 6 - highest die from a resistance roll)
- Special ability triggers (varies)

When the Stress track fills to 9: the PC is **Stressed Out** — they take a Trauma and reset to 0.

**Trauma** (max 4 of: Cold, Haunted, Obsessed, Paranoid, Reckless, Soft, Unstable, Vicious). At 4 Trauma, the PC retires from the dangerous life.

Stress recovers through downtime Indulge Vice activities; Trauma stays unless special abilities clear it.

entries.name: Mechanic: Resistance rolls
entries.content:
When the GM declares a consequence the PC doesn't want to accept, the PC can ROLL TO RESIST.

1. Pick the relevant attribute (Insight for mental / clever consequences; Prowess for physical / positional; Resolve for emotional / supernatural).
2. Roll Nd6 where N = current rating of that attribute (or 1d if rating is 0).
3. Look at the HIGHEST die.
4. Cost in Stress = 6 - highest die.
   - 6 = 0 stress (resist freely)
   - 5 = 1 stress
   - 4 = 2 stress
   - 3 = 3 stress
   - 2 = 4 stress
   - 1 = 5 stress
5. Critical (multiple 6s) = clear 1 stress instead.

The consequence is reduced in severity OR negated entirely (GM's call based on the roll).

entries.name: Mechanic: Harm levels
entries.content:
Harm is tracked at 3 levels with descriptive tags.

- **Level 1 (Lesser)**: Bruised, Scared, Off-balance. -0 dice (no effect on rolls).
- **Level 2 (Moderate)**: Battered, Drained, Furious. -1 die on any roll the harm interferes with.
- **Level 3 (Severe)**: Impaled, Terrified, Broken. PC cannot do the things that harm impedes; -1 to all rolls.
- **Level 4 (Death/Incapacitation)**: only reached if you can't resist; PC is taken out (unconscious / dying / catatonic).

When the PC takes a harm that would go to a row already filled, the existing harm escalates UP one level. Healing happens during downtime Recover activity.

entries.name: Mechanic: Playbooks (PC archetypes)
entries.content:
Blades uses 7 PC playbooks, each with distinct special abilities and starting Action distributions:

- **Cutter** — aggressive enforcer. Pressure / Battleborn.
- **Hound** — sharpshooter / scout. Sharpshooter / Sense of Doom.
- **Leech** — alchemist / tinkerer. Alchemist / Saboteur.
- **Lurk** — infiltrator. Ambush / Shadow.
- **Slide** — face / con artist. Like Looks / Cloak & Dagger.
- **Spider** — mastermind / fixer. Foresight / Connected.
- **Whisper** — occult specialist. Compel / Ghost Voice.

The Playbook field on the sheet header should be set to one of these. Each playbook unlocks unique downtime moves and special abilities purchased with XP.

entries.name: Mechanic: Score → Downtime loop
entries.content:
**Free Play** — open-ended scenes, gathering info, choosing the next score.

**Score** — a heist. GM picks the kind (Assault, Deception, Stealth, Occult, Social, Transport). Players pick the Plan (Plan archetype + Detail). Engagement Roll determines starting Position. Then the score plays out as a series of Action rolls.

**Downtime** — after a score. Each PC gets ≥2 downtime activities: Acquire Asset (gear / connection / leverage), Long-Term Project (work toward something multi-session), Recover (heal harm), Reduce Heat, Train (XP), Indulge Vice (clear stress). Then the cycle resumes with Free Play.

entries.name: Mechanic: Load (flexible gear)
entries.content:
Blades does NOT track gear pre-mission. Instead the PC declares their **Load** at score start:

- **Light (3)**: stealthy, fast. Fewer items available to flashback-equip.
- **Normal (5)**: balanced.
- **Heavy (6)**: combat-ready. Slower, more conspicuous.

During the score, the player **flashback-equips** items: "I have a smokebomb" — mark 1 Load if there's space. The Playbook's gear sheet lists what's available; tier and quality of gear gates effectiveness.

entries.name: Mechanic: Flashback (retroactive prep)
entries.content:
Player declares a flashback to establish that the PC prepared something earlier. Cost in Stress = GM's call based on plausibility:

- 0 stress — trivial ("I bought a torch in the market that morning").
- 1 stress — moderate ("I bribed the guard captain to look the other way").
- 2 stress — major ("I'd already broken into the safe and replaced the deed with a fake").

The flashback resolves with normal action rolls if needed. Mechanically equivalent to having done it in fiction, but unlocked retroactively.

entries.name: Mechanic: Crew and Heat
entries.content:
Players collectively run a **Crew** with its own playbook (Assassins, Bravos, Cult, Hawkers, Shadows, Smugglers). Crew tier scales contacts, reach, and starting Heat. 

**Heat** = how hot the score made the Crew with city authorities. Each score adds Heat based on: noisy / violent / killed (1-4 Heat). At 9+ Heat, the Crew gains a **Wanted Level**, attracting law-enforcement attention until reduced via downtime.

**Rep** = how the underworld knows the Crew. Earned each score; spent to advance Crew tier (more contacts, better gear, larger turf).

entries.name: Mechanic: Ghosts, Electroplasm, Occult
entries.content:
Doskvol setting flavor — ghosts are real and visible to those attuned. Electroplasm fuels the city. Spirit wells, lightning barriers, demon contracts.

**Attune** is the relevant skill. Ghost-touched PCs (Whisper playbook) can ritualize, see across the veil, and bargain with spirits. Long-Term Project: research a ritual; pays out in setting-specific occult power.

entries.name: Mechanic: XP and advancement
entries.content:
Each PC gets XP at the end of every score based on Playbook trigger fulfillment + general triggers (each Action category): "You addressed a challenge with desperate action or great effect" — 1 XP in the relevant attribute's category.

At 6 XP in a category, gain a new dot OR a Playbook special ability. Special abilities unlock new mechanical levers (e.g. Slide's 'Like Looks' lets them flashback to a charm play; Whisper's 'Ghost Voice' lets them communicate with spirits).

entries.name: Mechanic: Vice and Overindulgence
entries.content:
Every PC has a Vice (Faith, Gambling, Luxury, Obligation, Pleasure, Stupor, Weird). Each downtime, the PC may Indulge Vice to clear stress.

**Indulge roll**: 1d for each open Action box in the Vice's category, OR 1d minimum. The result clears that much stress.

**Overindulgence**: if the roll clears more stress than current, the PC has overindulged. GM picks a Vice complication: PC is Trapped, Brag, Lost, or Tapped (depending on Vice + result).

entries.name: Difficulty ladder reference
entries.content:
Blades in the Dark difficulty ladder. Threshold values needed for success:
- Controlled (threshold 0) — Position: you act from a position of advantage. Failure costs minor — withdraw, reduced Effect, a small complication.
- Risky (threshold 1) — Position: you act in the danger zone. Failure costs standard — harm, lost opportunity, escalating trouble.
- Desperate (threshold 2) — Position: you act under dire pressure. Failure costs severe — serious harm, lost objective, fatal complication.
- Limited (threshold 3) — Effect: even success advances the goal only slightly. Use when conditions or scale work against the PC.
- Standard (threshold 4) — Effect: success advances the goal as the player intended.
- Great (threshold 5) — Effect: success advances the goal further than expected — extra clock ticks, bonus information, broader impact.

customTools.name: blades_in_the_dark_reference
customTools.description: Returns canonical Blades in the Dark reference: dice, resolution, difficulty ladder, attributes, skills. Call when the GM needs authoritative ruleset detail mid-narration.
customTools.staticResult:
# Blades in the Dark — canonical reference

Forged in the Dark heist game. Roll N d6 = Action rating ± modifiers; highest die determines outcome — 6 = success, 4-5 = partial, 1-3 = failure, multiple 6s = critical. GM declares Position (Controlled / Risky / Desperate) and Effect (Limited / Standard / Great) BEFORE the roll; both axes negotiate before dice hit the table.

## Dice
- Type: `d6`
- Notation: `Nd6 take highest (Action rating dice pool)`

## Resolution
- Mode: `narrative-handled`

## Difficulty ladder
- **Controlled** (threshold 0) — Position: you act from a position of advantage. Failure costs minor — withdraw, reduced Effect, a small complication.
- **Risky** (threshold 1) — Position: you act in the danger zone. Failure costs standard — harm, lost opportunity, escalating trouble.
- **Desperate** (threshold 2) — Position: you act under dire pressure. Failure costs severe — serious harm, lost objective, fatal complication.
- **Limited** (threshold 3) — Effect: even success advances the goal only slightly. Use when conditions or scale work against the PC.
- **Standard** (threshold 4) — Effect: success advances the goal as the player intended.
- **Great** (threshold 5) — Effect: success advances the goal further than expected — extra clock ticks, bonus information, broader impact.

## Attributes
- **Insight** (INS) [Action Category] — Quick-thinking, perception, study. Covers Hunt / Study / Survey / Tinker Actions. Resists harm to mind / sense / cleverness — Insight resistance roll.
- **Prowess** (PRO) [Action Category] — Physical capability, stealth, combat. Covers Finesse / Prowl / Skirmish / Wreck Actions. Resists harm to body / position — Prowess resistance roll.
- **Resolve** (RES) [Action Category] — Willpower, conviction, social presence. Covers Attune / Command / Consort / Sway Actions. Resists harm to morale / heart / will — Resolve resistance roll.

## Skills
- **Hunt** — Track, ambush, attack at range. Catching prey before they escape.
- **Study** — Examine details, decipher meaning, recall lore. Reading a person or text.
- **Survey** — Observe a situation, predict events, look around. Casing a place.
- **Tinker** — Fiddle with devices, pick locks, disarm traps, modify gear.
- **Finesse** — Sleight of hand, riding, controlling vehicles, dueling with quick precision.
- **Prowl** — Sneak, hide, move quietly, parkour through unfamiliar terrain.
- **Skirmish** — Engage in close combat, fight savagely, brawl. Up-close violence.
- **Wreck** — Break things, demolish, sunder. Smashing through obstacles.
- **Attune** — Sense, channel, or contact the ghost field. Resist spectral / electroplasmic threats.
- **Command** — Intimidate, lead, demand obedience. Force someone to act against their preference.
- **Consort** — Socialize, schmooze, network. Negotiate from a position of mutual interest.
- **Sway** — Persuade, deceive, charm. Convince someone to see things your way.

additionalAgents.name: Blades in the Dark — Pre-Input Transformer
additionalAgents.description: Re-frames common player-input phrases in Blades in the Dark-native vocabulary before the narration model sees them.
additionalAgents.promptTemplate:
# Blades in the Dark — pre-input transformer

You are a pre-generation context-injection agent for an RPG roleplay using the Blades in the Dark ruleset overlay. Your job is to read the player's most recent input and emit a SHORT annotation that re-frames common verbs and action phrases in Blades in the Dark-flavored vocabulary.

## Output format

Emit a context-injection block of the form:
```
[blades-in-the-dark annotation] <one-sentence re-frame, OR "No re-frame needed.">
```

Annotate only when one of the recognized patterns matches the player's input. Otherwise output exactly "No re-frame needed." and stop.

## Recognition table

| When player input matches | Re-frame guidance |
|---|---|
| I attack / I fight / I shoot | In Blades: pick an Action rating (Skirmish for melee, Hunt for ranged, Finesse for fencing-style). Roll dice = rating. GM declares Position (Controlled/Risky/Desperate) and Effect (Limited/Standard/Great) BEFORE the roll. Highest die: 6 = success, 4-5 = partial (with cost), 1-3 = bad outcome. |
| I sneak / I hide / I prowl | Prowl action. GM should declare Position based on guards / lighting / distance. Effect typically Standard unless circumstances are unusual. |
| I push myself | Player gains +1d to the roll OR +1 Effect OR perform an action you couldn't otherwise (e.g. supernaturally hard feat). Cost: 2 Stress. Mark stress on the sheet. |
| I accept a devil's bargain | GM offers a complication (collateral damage, double-crossed friend, attention drawn) in exchange for +1d. Player accepts or declines. If accepted, the complication WILL happen regardless of roll. |
| I resist (a consequence) | After taking a consequence (harm, lost ground, breakdown), the player rolls the appropriate Attribute (Insight/Prowess/Resolve) as Nd6 take highest. 6 = avoid it entirely; 4-5 = reduce it; 1-3 = take it. Cost: 6 - highest die in Stress (so a 6 = 0 stress, a 1 = 5 stress). |
| I indulge my Vice | Downtime activity. Roll the lowest action rating in the Vice's category (or just 1d). Result = stress cleared. Watch for Overindulgence — rolling more dice than current stress can trigger a Vice complication. |

## Rules

- Match liberally (paraphrase / synonym OK) but never invent annotations for input that doesn't fit any row.
- Never narrate the scene; never speak as a character; never roll dice.
- One annotation per turn maximum. If multiple rows match, pick the most specific.
- Keep the annotation under 25 words.

scenarioDefault:
This chat uses the Blades in the Dark ruleset overlay.

Forged in the Dark heist game. Roll N d6 = Action rating ± modifiers; highest die determines outcome — 6 = success, 4-5 = partial, 1-3 = failure, multiple 6s = critical. GM declares Position (Controlled / Risky / Desperate) and Effect (Limited / Standard / Great) BEFORE the roll; both axes negotiate before dice hit the table.

Mechanics: primary die d6, notation Nd6 take highest (Action rating dice pool).
Resolution: narrative-handled.

The GM should keep narration consistent with Blades in the Dark mechanics. Refer to the lorebook entries for ruleset reference.
